prompt: A towering, imposing cliff face viewed from the bottom looking up. The lowest layers of the cliff are natural, ancient sedimentary rock. As the layers rise upward, the stone seamlessly transitions into terrifyingly dense, compressed geological strata made entirely of crushed electronic waste—millions of obsolete smartphones, tangled black wires, shattered screens, and green circuit boards crushed together like fossil layers. A tiny, solitary human figure stands at the base of the cliff, looking up at the overwhelming, suffocating scale of the technological debris. Photorealistic, National Geographic documentary photography, somber and dramatic lighting, highly textured. Absolutely no text, no labels, no charts, no graphs, no ui elements.
insight: Humanity is generating electronic waste five times faster than we are recycling it, creating an overwhelming, permanent geological footprint.
